summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of the new MatchMaker Premium upgrade flow (Razorpay-mocked). Backend: 22/22 pytest cases pass — pricing math (1999+18% GST=2358.82), 15-area expertise catalog, mock order initiation/verification, duplicate-order/already-premium rejection, advanced-data validation (missing primary identifiers, missing confidentiality, invalid area), file uploads (PDF/JPG/PNG/DOC/DOCX up to 5MB), oversized & invalid-extension rejection, soft-delete with 30-day purge & archive collection mirroring, audit log capture, GridFS bucket segregation (matchmaker_uploads), auth enforcement on all endpoints. Frontend: end-to-end flow on the deployed preview URL — Upgrade to Premium button on /matchmaker/experts/register?edit=1, hover popover with INR 1999 + GST 359.82 = 2358.82 breakdown, profile-save→navigate to /matchmaker/experts/premium, mock Razorpay 'Pay Now', auto-transition to Advanced Data step, Firm Name input, Primary Area dropdown (15 options), primary/secondary identifier cards with Upload+Delete buttons, confidentiality dialog with disabled-until-checked confirm button, redirect to dashboard, and Premium Profile Data section visible in the My Profile tab on ExpertDashboard. No blocking issues found.

issues: After redirect from premium flow, dashboard first shows the 'Welcome Back / confirm your profile' step. User must click 'Looks good — continue' to reach the My Profile tab where the new Premium Profile Data card is visible. Functionally correct, but new premium users may not realize the data is one click deeper. Consider deep-linking ?tab=profile after submit, or auto-skipping the welcome step when is_premium is already true.

critical_code_review_comments: POST /matchmaker/expert/premium/verify-payment in matchmaker_premium.py does NOT verify razorpay_signature (HMAC-SHA256). This is intentional per the mock spec, but a clear TODO/comment is in place; ensure real-key path is wired before production.
verify-payment trusts the client-supplied order_id only via {order_id, user_id} match — fine for the mock, but in production also bind to amount and idempotency token.
AdvancedDataPayload.primary_identifiers/secondary_identifiers are typed as plain dict — consider Dict[str, str] with length caps to prevent oversized payloads.
Soft-delete archive uses dict-spread of the live record then pops _id; safe, but consider a one-time periodic job to actually purge files beyond scheduled_purge_at (not yet implemented).
matchmaker_premium.py is ~430 lines and well-structured; no DRY violations detected.
Route imports VERIFICATION_FIELDS from expert_registration.py — no circular import; both routers register cleanly at startup.
test_credentials: Tests register fresh users via POST /api/auth/register (TEST_*@example.com / TestPass@123). admin@ipolabs.com was NOT used because it may already have a premium profile from earlier mock-upgrades.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates 1 TEST_premium_<uuid> user with 1 expert profile, 1 mock order, 1 premium profile, 1 PDF file (uploaded then soft-deleted), and ~5 audit log entries. All cleaned up via module-scoped autouse fixture using PyMongo. UI run also creates 2 short-lived TEST_uife_/TEST_dash3_ users (not auto-cleaned by Playwright; can be removed manually if needed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest file at /app/backend/tests/test_matchmaker_premium.py covers the full MatchMaker Premium API surface. UI flow on /matchmaker/experts/register?edit=1 → /matchmaker/experts/premium → /matchmaker/experts/dashboard works end-to-end with mocked Razorpay (1.2s setTimeout in ExpertPremium.jsx). GridFS bucket name is 'matchmaker_uploads' — verified via direct PyMongo query that matchmaker_uploads.files holds the doc and fs.files does not. New collections seen in MongoDB after a run: matchmaker_premium_profiles, matchmaker_audit_log, matchmaker_payment_orders, matchmaker_identifier_files, matchmaker_deleted_archive, matchmaker_uploads.files, matchmaker_uploads.chunks.
